name: A B M Muntasir Rahman
bio: A B M Muntasir Rahman is currently serving as a Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering department under the School of Data and Sciences at BRAC University. He holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, where he graduated with the highest distinction. During his undergraduate years, Muntasir served as a Student Tutor at the Computer Science and Engineering department of BRAC University, a role he held from June 2021 to December 2022. Additionally, he worked as a Student Mentor, providing guidance and assistance to fresher students in making the most of the resources and opportunities available at BRAC University. Following his graduation, Muntasir worked as a Lecturer in the department of Information Technology & Management (ITM) at Daffodil International University. His primary areas of research interest encompass Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Image Processing. Google Scholar | ResearchGate | LinkedIn

bio: Anika Priodorshinee Mrittika has been working as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the School of Data and Sciences at BRAC University since May 2024. She earned her Bachelor’s degree from the same department with the highest distinction. Prior to her current role, she spent nearly two years as Student Tutor and Adjunct Lecturer, combined, in the CSE department of BRAC University. At BRAC University, Ms. Mrittika currently teaches Numerical Methods theory and lab, Digital Logic Design theory, and the Human-Computer Interface lab. She is deeply motivated by a strong desire to make a meaningful impact through education, aiming to create an environment that fosters continuous learning and personal growth.

name: Ariyan Hossain
bio: Ariyan Hossain is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering from Brac University, graduating with the highest distinction. In his role as a Lecturer, Ariyan teaches core courses such as Data Structures (CSE220), Algorithms (CSE221) and an elective course Natural Language Processing (CSE440), actively engaging with students through both theory and lab sessions. Ariyan also served as a Graduate Research Assistant in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. Under the supervision of Dr. Muhammad Nur Yanhaona, he worked on classifying news article framing using machine learning and natural language processing techniques. His research interests include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and AI ethics. His undergraduate thesis, supervised by Dr. Farig Yousuf Sadeque, focused on exploring and mitigating gender bias in word embeddings from transformer-based language models, with the findings submitted for publication. Ariyan continues to contribute to academic research while fostering a passion for teaching and mentorship.

name: Arnob Saha Ankon
bio: Mr. Arnob Saha Ankon is currently serving as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the School of Data and Sciences, BRAC University. He completed his Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and Engineering from the Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET). For his undergraduate thesis, Mr. Arnob focused on computer security, specifically conducting a security analysis of REST APIs. His academic interests include bioinformatics, machine learning, cryptography, and recent work in computer vision. Prior to his current role at BRAC University, Mr. Arnob served as an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BUET. Outside of his academic duties, he enjoys gaming, watching movies and series, and is actively involved in research discussions. He is also passionate about mentoring and supporting the development of future tech professionals.

name: Asif Shahriar
bio: Asif Shahriar is currently working as a Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering department of BRAC University. He completed his B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2024, with a strong academic record. During his third year, Asif completed a hybrid internship at North West Power Generation Company Limited, where he helped develop a machine learning algorithm for predicting faulty ultrasonic flow-meters. His current research interests span across automated computer and network protocol security frameworks, natural language processing models, and Trustworthy LLMs. Alongside raw-performance improvement, he also values efficiency, modularity, and automation (what good are agents if not automated?). Asif is grateful to all the mentors and supervisors who have guided and supported him throughout his academic and research journey. He believes in “persistence until improvement”, even if it requires going the extra mile – and hopes his students would, too.
researchAreas: Secure & Interpretable LLMs for Communication Protocols (5G and beyond)

name: Ayesha Siddika
bio: Ayesha Siddika is a dedicated educator and computer science enthusiast, currently serving as an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University since January 2024. Her dedication to education is evident from her previous roles as a Student Tutor in both the Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) and Mathematics and Natural Sciences (MNS) departments at BRAC University from February 2021 to April 2022 and February 2020 to January 2021, respectively. Ayesha's commitment to academic excellence is underscored by her numerous accolades during her undergraduate years, where she graduated with the Highest Distinction in 2022 and received recognition on the Vice Chancellor's Honor List for outstanding results in seven semesters. She also achieved a coveted spot on the Dean's Honor List for exceptional performance in one semester, along with consistently earning Merit Scholarships based on her academic achievements in various semesters from Fall 2019 to Spring 2022. Currently pursuing her Master's degree in Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Ayesha continues to excel in her academic pursuits. Her passion extends to the realms of Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and active research, reflecting her keen interest in staying at the forefront of technological advancements. Ayesha's dynamic engagement with emerging technologies positions her as a valuable contributor to both academia and the field of Computer Science.

name: Ayon Roy
bio: Ayon Roy is an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Dhaka. He graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University in February 2025, graduating with the highest distinction in his program. Before joining BRAC University as an Adjunct Lecturer, Ayon held several technical roles. He completed a Digitalist Internship in the AI ​​and Automation Department at Banglalink Digital Communications Limited and worked as an Digital Finance and Services Intern with the ML Team at NCC Bank PLC. During his studentship, he also worked as a Undergraduate Student Tutor/ Teaching Assistant in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, supporting core academic subjects. Ayon's undergraduate thesis focused on the advancement of Bangla machine translation using informal datasets. This work involved creating a state-of-the-art dataset and applying sequence-to-sequence models to improve translation quality for low-resource and informal regional languages. His current research interests include Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), LLM, Cryptography.

name: Dewan Ziaul Karim
bio: Dewan Ziaul Karim (DZK) is a Senior Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. He completed his M.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, having initially started the program at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET). After completing his coursework at BUET, he transferred his credits to BRAC University to conduct his thesis and successfully earn his degree. He holds a B.Sc. in CSE from Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology (AUST). Before joining BRAC University as a lecturer, Mr. Karim served as a full-time lecturer at Daffodil International University and as a part-time lecturer at Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology . He currently also serves as the coordinator of the ITEE training program at BRAC University, where he is responsible for scheduling training sessions and organizing seminars. At BRAC University, he is currently teaching the Digital Logic Design theory and lab courses. Over the years, he has taught several undergraduate courses including Introduction to Computer Science , Data Communications , Database Management Systems , Software Engineering , and System Analysis and Design . He also regularly supervises final-year thesis and project work. Mr. Karim’s primary research interests lie in computer vision and machine learning , with applications in medical imaging , agriculture , and social impact sectors . He is driven by the goal of advancing visual recognition systems through deep learning and solving real-world problems with innovative, practical solutions. His long-term vision is to contribute to research that meaningfully impacts society and inspires future generations of engineers.

name: Dr. Amitabha Chakrabarty
bio: Professor Chakrabarty currently holds the position of a Computer Science and Engineering Professor at Brac University. Previously, he worked at the Independent University, Bangladesh (IUB), overseeing IT infrastructures. His educational journey includes a Ph.D. earned from Dublin City University (DCU) in 2012, complemented by B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees from the University of Rajshahi's Department of Computer Science and Engineering. Additionally, he pursued a second M.Sc. in Telecommunication Engineering at Independent University, Bangladesh. His research interests span diverse areas such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Computer Networking, Embedded Systems, and Switching Theory. With a prolific publication record of over 100 research papers across national and international journals and conferences, Professor Chakrabarty contributes significantly to his field. Beyond academia, he actively participates in various project consultations in development sectors. He also serves as a jury member in national IT competitions and serves as a reviewer for various international conferences and journals.

name: Dr. Aniqua Nusrat Zereen
bio: Aniqua Zereen completed her PhD in Computer Science from Asian Institute of Technology, Thailand, in 2023. She worked as a Research Assistant at the Artificial Intelligence Center at AIT for over 2 years and successfully completed a national project for 'NBTC' in Thailand. She also worked as a co-investigator for a project at Mahidol University, Thailand. Aniqua is a recipient of the Bangabandhu Science and Technology Fellowship Trust. Before joining AIT as a PhD student, she served as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University from 2013 to 2017. Additionally, she worked as a summer student at CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, in 2013.

name: Dr. Chowdhury Mofizur Rahman
bio: Professor Dr.  Chowdhury Mofizur Rahman did his Ph.D in Machine Learning back in 1996 from Tokyo Institute of Technology, one of the finest institutions of Japan. He did his Masters and Bachelors from BUET in 1992 and 1989 respectively. He served BUET as a faculty of CSE Department for about 15 years and during his tenure in BUET, he served the CSE department as the head from 1999 to 2001. After taking early retirement from BUET, in 2003 he founded United International University (UIU) with the financial patronage of United Group. He worked there as a Pro VC for about 15 years and he served the same institution as the Vice Chancellor from 2018 to 2022. On 15 th May, 2023 he started working for BRAC University as a professor of CSE Department in the school of Data and Sciences. After working at BRACU for one trimester, he joined State University of Bangladesh (SUB) as Vice Chancellor (Designate). After serving SUB one year, Professor Rahman came back to BRACU CSE department and since then he has been working at BRACU as a professor of CSE department till to date.

name: Dr. Farida Chowdhury
bio: Dr. Farida Chowdhury is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, BRAC University since July 2023. Previously she was a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Sylhet. She completed her PhD in Computing Science from the University of Stirling , UK. Her PhD thesis title was “Structured Peer-to-Peer Overlays for NATed Churn Intensive Networks”. She completed her MSc. in Network and e-Business Centred Computing which was an Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's Programme between the University of Reading, UK ; Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, Greece and Universidad Carlos(III) de Madrid, Spain . She is one of the authors of a book titled Digital Technology (ডিজিটাল প্রযুক্তি) of Class VI of National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB), Bangladesh. She was a visiting research scientist at the Fraunhofer Institute for Applied Information Technology (Fraunhofer FIT) , Germany from May, 2022 to August, 2023. During her SUST tenure, she also worked as the Coordinator of Pipilika, the first Bengali search engine, in between August, 2016 to December, 2019. Her current research interests include Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Security Usability, Privacy and Networking. She published numerous research articles in reputed international academic journals, conferences and workshops.

name: Dr. Farig Yousuf Sadeque
bio: Dr. Farig Sadeque is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. Before joining BRAC University, Dr. Sadeque was an Associate Research Scientist at the Educational Testing Service (ETS), the largest private non-profit educational testing organization in the world. He also worked as a postdoctoral research fellow at Harvard University where he was jointly appointed by the Harvard Medical School and Boston Children’s Hospital, and was a part of the Computational Health Informatics Program (CHIP) Laboratory. Dr. Sadeque completed his PhD in Information Science with a minor in Computer Science from The University of Arizona under the supervision of Dr. Steven Bethard. He completed his undergrad from Bangladesh University of Engineering in Technology, with a major in Computer Science and Engineering. His research interests include Computational Linguistics and Applied Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning, Computational Social Science, Psycholinguisitcs and Sociolinguistics.

name: Dr. Md Sadek Ferdous
bio: Dr. Ferdous is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. He is also an Honorary Research Fellow at the Institute for Security Science and Technology of Imperial College London. Previously, he was an ERCIM (European Research Consortium for Informatics and Mathematics) Alain Bensoussan Research Fellow at Fraunhofer Institute for Applied Information Technology (FIT), Sankt Augustin, Germany. During this one-year research fellowship he researched on Self-sovereign Identity Management using Blockchain Technology. He was supervised by Prof. Wolfgang Prinz. Previously, he was an Assistant Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Sylhet Bangladesh. During my SUST tenure, I established the first-ever blockchain-focused security research laboratory, called SUBSEL (Shahjalal University Blockchain and SEcurity Laboratory), in Bangladesh at SUST. Before joining SUST I worked as a Research Associate at the Centre for Financial Technology of the Imperial College Business School, Imperial College London, UK. I was involved in a project in collaboration with Citi Group to explore how the blockchain technology could be leveraged to improve email security for any global financial institution. I am still associated with the Centre to continue this research remotely from Bangladesh. While at Imperial, Dr. Ferdous had the opportunity to work at the UK Cabinet Office as their first Technology Fellow. He was a part of a joint-project involving the UK Cabinet Office, UK Ministry of Justice and UK Police in which I explored how the blockchain technology could be leveraged for securing digital evidences. He designed and developed a proof of concept which the UK ministry of Justice exploring the possibility for real-life trial. This has been a very successful project and it received world-wide media attention. Please check the Media link to view a few media coverages. He worked as a Research Fellow at the Electronics and Computer Science of the University of Southampton in a Horizon 2020 cloud security project called SUNFISH in between September, 2015 to June, 2017. In this project, it was investigated how the blockchain technology could be exploited to increase the security in a cloud federation. He was a Post-doctoral Research Assistant at the School of Computing Science of the University of Glasgow in a project called the Integrated Multimedia City Data (iMCD). This was part of an ESRC funded Urban Big Data Centre, Glasgow under the umbrella of Big Data Theme. He was responsible for conducting research and developing systems for collating a large collection of textual and multimedia data from online news sources, microblogs, social sharing sites such as Youtube & flickr and wearable sensors such as life-loggers and GPS devices and then to analyse this data set to make sense.

name: Dr. Md. Ashraful Alam
bio: Dr. Md. Ashraful Alam is working as an Associate Professor and the Director of CVIS Research Lab (Computer Vision and Intelligent Systems) at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering in BRAC University. Prior to joining BRAC University, Dr. Alam was working as a Post-Doc. at the School of Information and Communication Engineering in Chungbuk National University, South Korea. He received his Doctor of Engineering degree in Computer and Communication Engineering from the same university in February 2015. During his PhD program, he had been awarded “BK21 Best Student Award” in 2014 for his outstanding achievements as a BK21 scholar. Dr. Alam has published more than 100 papers in reputed international journals and conference proceedings. Dr. Alam's research interests include computer vision, image processing, machine learning, deep learning, machine vision, 3-D vision, medical data and image analytics, virtual and augmented reality. He is also engaged as Reviewer, Editorial Board Member, and Lead Guest Editor (Special issue) in reputed international journals. name: Dr. Md. Golam Rabiul Alam
bio: Md. Golam Rabiul Alam is a Professor at the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. He received Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyung Hee University, South Korea in 2017. He served as a Post-doctoral researcher in Computer Science and Engineering Department, at Kyung Hee University, Korea from March 2017 to February 2018. Dr. Rabiul Alam received B.S. and M.S. degrees in Computer Science and Engineering, and Information Technology from Khulna University and University of Dhaka respectively.   Dr. Rabiul Alam has published around 170 research articles in reputed journals, and conference proceedings. He has also 3 registered patents on ambient assisted living, mobile cloud computing, and mobile fog computing, respectively. Dr. Rabiul Alam's research interests include Mental-health informatics, Ambient intelligence systems, Mobile-cloud computing, Edge computing, Affective computing, and UAV Image processing.  He is a member of the IEEE Computer Society and received several best paper awards at national and international research conferences.

name: Dr. Md. Khalilur Rahman
bio: Dr. Md. Khalilur Rahman is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. He was the principal investigator of Nation’s first Nano-satellite program which is named as “BRAC ONNESHA” by BRAC University. He was assigned as the Acting Chairman of CSE department from 1st Sep-14 to 31st Dec-2014. He is the founder of Nano-Satellite Technology Research (NASTER) Lab, BRAC University Robotics lab and the founder advisor of BRAC University Robotics club. The robot named CHONDROBOT, MONGOL-TORI and BRACU-DUBURI was designed and built under his peer supervision. Different versions of these Robots are regularly attending in different international competitions such as NASA Lunabotic Mining Competition USA, University Rover Challenge USA, Singapore Autonomous Underwater Vehicle Competition etc. Dr. Khalil has joined in BRAC University on 2009 immediately after completing his PhD degree from Kyushu Institute of Technology, Japan and promoted as associate professor on 2013. He was a MONBUKAGAKUSHO scholar and his PhD Research topic was Natural Language Processing. But now his research is diverted into Space Science & Engineering, Robotics and Embedded Systems. He was born on 1976 in a river bank city Sirajganj of Bangladesh. After completing his Secondary and Higher Secondary Schooling he took admission in Institute of Science and Technology under National University for graduation and post-graduation degrees. Dr. Khalil became point of contact (POC) of UNISEC-Global (University Space Engineering Consortium) on 2014. In his research and work he could build a very good relationship with government organizations like BTRC, SPARRSO, Atomic Energy Commission, CARGS, ICT Ministry etc. He is also a technical committee member of a Number of International conferences and Journals. He is an IEEE member. S21KT is his amateur radio call sign.

name: Dr. Md. Sakir Hossain
bio: Dr. Md. Sakir Hossain is currently serving as an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science at BRAC University, Bangladesh. Prior to this, he held the same position in the Department of Computer Science at American International University-Bangladesh (AIUB). Dr. Hossain is an accomplished academic and researcher with expertise in communications, cybersecurity, and signal processing. He earned his Ph.D. in the field of Wireless Networks from Saitama University, Japan, and received both his B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees in Information and Communication Engineering from the University of Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He also conducted postdoctoral research at the Czech Technical University in Prague, Czech Republic. His research interests cover a broad range of topics, including applied machine learning, cybersecurity, medical signal processing, and wireless networks. Dr. Hossain is a Senior Member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers ( IEEE), USA.

name: Dr. Mohammad Kaykobad
bio: Professor Kaykobad served as a Distinguished Professor of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. He retired from CSE Department, BUET in 2020.  He was awarded the Gold Medal for contribution to ICT Education by the Bangladesh Computer Society in 2005. In 2006 he won the Bangladesh Academy of Sciences Gold Medal for Physical Sciences Senior Group. He was one of the  pioneers in introducing competitive programming in Bangladesh. He led the BUET team to the ACM Finals numerous times and his 2000 team finished 11th in the world. He received the Outstanding Coach Award at the ACM ICPC Finals held in Honolulu, Hawaii in 2002. In 2013 he was awarded the Senior Coach Award at the ACM ICPC Finals in St. Petersburg. In 2019 he was awarded ICPC Foundation Life Coach Award at Porto, Portugal. He is one of the pioneers in introducing Mathematics, Informatics and Science   Olympiads   in Bangladesh. He has worked as a Director of the Dhaka and Chittagong Stock Exchanges for many years and is a member of the Academic Councils and Syndicates of several universities. He is currently an Independent Director of Sonali Bank. Dr. Kaykobad was a Visiting Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of Hong Kong during the years 2000 and 2001, KAIST, Korea, Monash University Gippsland Campus, Australian National University, Amritapuri University, India Kyong Hee University, Korea. He is keen to popularize science and develop analytical skill in Bangladeshi students. He is a very frequent column writer inspiring educational excellence and patriotism in young people. Dr Kaykobad is a Fellow of Bangladesh Academy of Sciences.

name: Dr. Muhammad Iqbal Hossain
bio: Dr. Muhammad Iqbal Hossain is an Associate Professor of the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. Significant achievements and rigorous pursuits mark his academic journey. Dr. Hossain earned his Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyungpook National University, South Korea, in January 2018. During his doctoral tenure, he actively participated in the Embedded Software Engineering Lab. His contributions to this lab resulted in a substantial collection of peer-reviewed journals and presentations at both international and domestic conferences in Korea. Dr. Hossain's commitment to staying at the forefront of his field is evident through his active participation in various international conferences and symposiums, which have enriched his perspectives and insights. Dr. Hossain's scholarly pursuits have garnered him recognition beyond his academic accomplishments. His expertise has led him to assume significant roles as a Reviewer and Editorial Board Member for reputable international journals and conferences. Moreover, his remarkable academic journey was facilitated by prestigious scholarships, including the Korean Government Scholarship (GKS) in 2009, which enabled his graduate studies. Subsequently, his exceptional dedication and contributions were acknowledged with the KNU Honors Scholarship during his doctoral pursuit. Dr. Iqbal’s research interests primarily focus on Information security (Including Cryptography, Access control, and privacy), AI and Machine learning, Model-based Software Engineering, Software Testing and Verification, and automation. He continues to supervise research projects and mentor students, having overseen over thirty undergraduate projects and more than twenty master’s theses. Additionally, he led the Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) at UIU from 2022 to 2023 and founded Bangladesh’s first undergraduate Data Science program.

name: Faria Binta Awal
bio: Faria Binta Awal is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University and an Adjunct Lecturer at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering , Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) . She earned her B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BUET in March 2025, where she graduated with distinction, ranking among the top 10 students in her cohort. Her research focuses on developing computational methods for life science, spatial biology, and biomedical data science. She has worked on projects spanning machine learning for spatial transcriptomics, graph-based biological modeling, and deep learning for biomedical imaging. In undergraduate thesis, Faria developed SuNSTAMP, a supervised factorization framework integrating spatial transcriptomics with complementary molecular readouts, and contributed to the Spatialomics-Toolkit, presented at the Festival of Genomics & Biodata Boston 2025 workshop. She has received multiple academic and research awards, including the RISE (Research and Innovation Centre for Science and Engineering) Research Grant and national recognition in programming contests such as the National Girls’ Programming Contest (Champion, 2022). Her team (BUET_Harmonica) ranked 23 rd in ICPC Asia Dhaka Regional Contest 2024. Her ongoing work explores graph-based contrastive learning and computational models of cell–cell communication in regenerative biology.

bio: I am an aspiring physicist with an MSc in Particles, Strings and Cosmology from Durham University. My MSc. was funded by the Hatfield Lioness Scholarship which I was awarded from a pool of around 500 applicants in 2020. After completion of my MSc. I resumed my role as a lecturer at the Computer Science and Engineering Department at Brac University in Bangladesh. I have instructed courses on Artificial Intelligence, Simulation and Modeling and Statistics. Currently, I am also conducting a course on Probabilistic Models. Majoring both in Physics and Computer Science in my bachelors (BRAC University), I am also enthusiastic about data science, AI and simulation. Especially the applications of these fields in modern day physics and solving social and practical problems intrigue me. Recently I have supervised thesis projects on Application of Physics Informed Neural Networks in Cosmological Parameter Predictions and, Use of Quantum Aware Algorithms in Image Protection in Generative AI. I am passionate about equity in STEAM and want to contribute to building a community of students studying fundamental sciences.  I have initiated and coordinated the participation of my home country, Bangladesh in the European Girls’ Mathematical Olympiad and have been the team leader/deputy leader in 2024, 2022 and 2021. In 2023, I started Bangladesh Girls' Mathematics Foundation aiming to empower girls of Bangladesh in Mathematics and STEM fields.

bio: K.M. Shadman Wadith is a lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. Before this, he was a lecturer at the Green University of Bangladesh. He completed his B.Sc. from the Department of CSE, University of Dhaka. His research focus is Reinforcement Learning, particularly Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). He has dedicated his career to exploring scalable, safe, and adaptive solutions for autonomous systems. His research includes privacy-preserving, decentralized MARL algorithms, and his master's research investigates hybrid MARL approaches that bridge offline and online paradigms for adaptive decision-making in dynamic environments. Alongside his core research, his interests extend to trustworthy and robust AI and NLP, with specific challenges in creating a Bangla language model.

name: Mahbubul Alam Majumdar, PhD
bio: I am a mathematician with a wide array of interests. My interests in computer science are development oriented -- how can we use computer science techniques and computational power to make the lives of Bangladeshis better?  My academic interests are in string theory, early universe cosmology, quantum computing and machine learning. For those wanting to work with me or do a thesis, an outline of my interests is below. One of my primary goals is the guiding of  the best mathematical and scientific talent in Bangladesh and the creation of a community of extremely capable Bangladeshi mathematicians, theoretical physicists and computer scientists. I am MIT's Regional Coordinator and the National Coach of the Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad Team. I try to contribute to nation-building by injecting technical sophistication into the business and financial spaces and supporting entrepreneurs by finding them financial support and providing technical expertise to turn their ideas into successful companies to create a modern Bangladesh. I have taught a wide array of courses ranging from Machine Learning to Number Theory to Quantum Field Theory.

name: Md. Akhtaruzaman Adnan
bio: Md Akhtaruzzaman Adnan is a Senior Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, where he makes significant contributions to teaching and research. With over a decade of academic experience, he previously served as a faculty member at the University of Asia Pacific for 11 years, nurturing the next generation of computer scientists and engineers. Adnan holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), one of Southeast Asia’s leading institutions, and a Bachelor’s degree from the Islamic University of Technology (IUT) in Gazipur. His academic background provides a strong foundation in both the theoretical and applied aspects of computer science. His research interests encompass Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Cybersecurity in Healthcare settings, Natural Language Processing, and Algorithm Design. He has authored several publications in reputed journals and conferences. His scholarly contributions can be explored through his Google Scholar profile , which reflects his commitment to advancing knowledge in these fields. Akhtaruzzaman’s dedication to academia is matched by his passion for mentoring students and fostering a vibrant research environment. His work aims to bridge the gap between academia and industry by promoting practical, impactful solutions to real-world problems. Beyond academia, he is actively involved in competitive programming and tech community development. He has played a pivotal role in organizing prestigious programming contests such as the International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC) and the National Collegiate Programming Contest (NCPC). Notably, he was one of the lead organizers of the ICPC World Finals 2022, hosted in Dhaka, Bangladesh, by UAP—a landmark event in the global programming community.

name: Md. Asif Haider
bio: Md. Asif Haider served as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University from 2024-25. He earned his BSc. Engg. degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2024. During his undergraduate studies, Asif completed two short, hybrid internships at OpenRefactory Bangladesh Limited and North West Power Generation Company Limited, where he focused on Secure Software Engineering and Machine Learning tasks, respectively. Additionally, he had actively mentored high school students for the past few years. His research interests span Software Engineering, Large Language Models, and Human Factors in Computing and AI. He is also passionate about Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing techniques applied to the Healthcare domain, as well as contemporary topics in computational social science. Asif is deeply thankful for the mentors and resources he has been blessed with throughout his journey. Driven by a strong enthusiasm for research and development, he is committed to contributing to the academic community with what students need most: quality education, right guidance, and motivating support. He is also an advocate of academic and cultural leadership activities on campus and beyond. When he is not busy with academic/research work, he is likely to be found exploring music, literature, and theaters. Starting in fall 2025, Asif continues to work as a Graduate Teaching Assistant at the University of California, Irvine while on study leave to pursue his PhD.

name: Md. Fakhruddin Gazzali
bio: MD. Fakhruddin Gazzali is a respected lecturer in Computer Science and Engineering Dept. at BRAC University. He earned his Bachelor of Science degree (BSc.) in the same field from the Islamic University of Technology (IUT), demonstrating his commitment to his discipline from the outset of his career. At BRAC University, Mr. Gazzali is currently teaching courses in Algorithms, Data Communication, and Object Oriented Programming. These subjects are fundamental to the field of Computer Science and Engineering, and his expertise in these areas greatly benefits his students. Before his current role at BRAC, Mr. Gazzali served as a lecturer at United International University (UIU), where he taught a range of these subjects including Structured Programming Language. His teaching prowess was particularly evident in his DBMS and Algorithm course, where all the teams he guided became champions or achieved other top honors, reflecting his effective mentorship and the quality of his instruction. In addition to his academic roles, Mr. Gazzali has also gained practical industry experience as a Software Developer Intern at Nilavo Technologies. This experience has enriched his teaching and research, providing him with valuable insights into the practical applications of computer science. Beyond his professional responsibilities, Mr. Gazzali is an active volunteer with the Mozilla Bangladesh Organization and the Firefox Social Support Team, demonstrating his commitment to the broader tech community. Mr. Gazzali's research interests span Software Engineering, Computer Vision, and Computer Networks. His work in these areas is driven by his passion for exploring new frontiers in technology. Further attesting to his expertise, Mr. Gazzali holds a CCNA certificate, a globally recognized IT certification. His diverse interests and accomplishments reflect his dedication to continuous learning and contribution to the field of computer science.

name: Md. Saiful Bari Siddiqui
bio: Md. Saiful Bari Siddiqui (Iftu) is a passionate educator and researcher with a strong background in engineering and computer science. His personality type is ENTP-A . His constant tendency to question the establishment and norms along with his expressive, headstrong, and independent nature characterize him as a person. Saiful holds a B.Sc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) , and an M.Sc in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University . During his undergraduate studies, he actively participated in extracurricular activities, including debating and leadership roles. He acted as the Vice President of the BUET Debating Club and was a member of BUETDC-Nandonik from 2018 to 2020, winning two national championships. He also has a keen interest in logical reasoning and problem-solving, and he has excelled in various academic pursuits, particularly olympiads. He has been a National and Divisional winner in the Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad and Bangladesh Physics Olympiad. During the lockdown in 2020, he got familiar with the field of Data Science and Machine Learning through online platforms and self-study. This sparked the beginning of his research career in Applied Machine Learning and Deep Learning. Bioinformatics, Digital Signal Processing, Image Processing, and other facets of Computer Vision are also among his research interests. Currently, he is involved in multiple projects focusing on Medical Imaging and Bioinformatics. In August of 2021, he began teaching at BRAC University. Since joining Brac University, he has taught a variety of courses including Image Processing (CSE428), and coordinated the CSE250 Lab (Circuits & Electronics). He is also a passionate sports enthusiast and a die-hard admirer of both Real Madrid and Bangladesh Cricket. The exciting areas of Bioinformatics and Sports Analytics are where he hopes to use his expertise in the future.

bio: Hello! I am Saiful Islam, someone who is passionate about teaching and is currently enrolled as a Senior lecturer at BRAC University. My love for teaching has transcended classrooms and has been depicted on platforms like YouTube ( https://www.youtube.com/@saifulcreates ) which I created for the sole purpose of inspiring keen learners around the world. Through my tutorials and study materials, I  hope to live my dreams through my students and ensure education is accessible to all. Research Area(s): Google scholar profile I am wholeheartedly dedicated to nurture my students to achieve global milestones. My unwavering commitment behind the success of my students has earned me accolades like a Gold Medal at the International Science and Invention Fair organized by the Young Scientists Association. Besides, I have served as a Co-Principal Investigator at BRACU Mongol Tori and was selected to participate in NASA’s Mars Rover Competition that took place in Utah, USA. Additionally, I have served as the Co-Advisor of the Robotics Club. Together, all these experiences make me want to foster innovation and creativity amongst all students. At the same time, my leadership as a team leader of Techniche, a fully-funded Techno-Management Festival at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), showcases my organizational acumen and effective leadership qualities. This role contributed to securing a merit position at the prestigious Indian Rover Challenge (IRC), organized by Vellore Institute of Technology (VIT), Chennai, India. Apart from the above mentioned, my other contributions have been featured in several prominent news sources, for example - Mars Society South Asia , The Daily Star, BRACU Express .

name: Mr. Moin Mostakim
bio: I am Moin Mostakim , an individual driven by a deep passion for learning and building knowledge. Throughout my life, I have always been eager to stay up-to-date with cutting-edge technology and immerse myself in the world of research. My journey as a neural network architecture specialist and a blissful educator has been a fulfilling one, marked by continuous growth and exploration. From an early age, I discovered my insatiable curiosity for understanding how things work. This innate curiosity led me to dive into the realm of technology, where I quickly became captivated by the intricate workings of neural networks. The ability of these systems to mimic the human brain and process complex information fascinated me beyond measure. As I embarked on my educational journey, I dedicated myself to mastering the field of neural network architecture. I pursued a rigorous academic path, obtaining a degree in Computer Science and specialising in artificial intelligence. The more I delved into the subject, the more captivated I became by the immense potential of neural networks to revolutionise various domains, from healthcare to finance and beyond. Driven by a relentless pursuit of knowledge, I embarked on numerous research projects, seeking to push the boundaries of what was possible in the field of neural networks. I embraced the challenges that arose, from designing innovative architectures to optimising performance and tackling complex problems. Through these research endeavours, I honed my skills and gained invaluable insights into the intricacies of neural network development.

name: Mr. Shakir Rouf
bio: With rapid technological progress in the Internet of Things (IoT), it has become imperative to concentrate on its security aspect. This paper represents a model that accounts for the detection of botnets through the use of machine learning algorithms. The model examined anomalies, commonly referred to as botnets, in a cluster of IoT devices attempting to connect to a network. Essentially, this paper exhibited the use of transport layer data (User Datagram Protocol - UDP) generated through IoT devices. An intelligent novel model comprising Random Forest Classifier with Independent Component Analysis (ICA) was proposed for botnet detection in IoT devices. Various machine learning algorithms were also implemented upon the processed data for comparative analysis. The experimental results of the proposed model generated state-of-the-art results for three different datasets, achieving up to 99.99% accuracy effectively with the lowest prediction time of 0.12 seconds without overfitting. The significance of this study lies in detecting botnets in IoT devices effectively and efficiently under all circumstances by utilizing ICA with Random Forest Classifier, which is a simple machine learning algorithm.

Dedicated to academic research, Niloy has made significant contributions in the Natural Language Processing (NLP) field. His current research interests include Applied Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning and Deep Learning.

name: Redowanul Akbar
bio: Redowanul Akbar is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh. He completed his bachelor’s degree in computer science and engineering in 2023 and joined BRAC University as an adjunct faculty member in Spring 2024. Later, in Summer 2024, he was appointed as a full-time lecturer of BRAC University. His research interests span human - computer interaction, reinforcement learning, large language models, and robotics, with a particular emphasis on human-centered and hardware-aware intelligent systems. He is especially interested in applying machine learning and reinforcement learning techniques to real-world problems involving decision-making, autonomy, and system optimization across both software and embedded platforms. Alongside his research activities, Redowanul Akbar is actively involved in teaching and curriculum development. He has taught courses including CSE340: Computer Architecture, CSE360: Computer Interfacing, CSE461: Introduction to Robotics, and embedded systems fundamentals. He is known for integrating theoretical foundations with hands-on experimentation, encouraging students to bridge the gap between abstract concepts and practical implementation. Beyond academia, he is keen on interdisciplinary innovation that connects technological research with societal needs. His long-term academic goal is to pursue advanced research and contribute to impactful teaching and scholarship in computer science and engineering.

name: Sadif Ahmed
bio: Sadif Ahmed is currently serving as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. He earned his Bachelor of Science (BSc. Engg.) degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2025 He has served as a Research Assistant at BUET, where he worked on Software Engineering Automation and Web UI automation , contributing to projects that enhance the reliability and security of software systems through intelligent tooling. During his undergraduate studies, Sadif also conducted impactful research on secret breach detection in source code and issue reports using Large Language Models . He has developed a wide range of practical tools and platforms in areas like blockchain, home automation, and anime data indexing. His research interests span Software Engineering and Security , Large Language Models , and Developer Privacy . He is particularly passionate about using Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing techniques to advance software quality, reliability, and confidentiality. Sadif is grateful for the academic mentors and collaborative opportunities that have supported his journey. Driven by a strong enthusiasm for both research and development, he aims to contribute meaningfully to the academic and software engineering communities. Outside of academics, he enjoys sports, gaming and watching anime and tv shows.

name: Towshik Anam Taj
bio: Mr. Towshik Anam Taj completed both his undergraduate and postgraduate degree from BRAC University in Computer Science and Computer Science and Engineering respectively. He has been awarded the prestigious Vice-Chancellor’s Gold Medal recently for his performance in postgraduate studies. He has served the university as a student tutor which is equivalent to undergraduate teaching assistant during his undergraduate days and was awarded with certification by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering for his services. Mr. Towshik Anam Taj has also worked as a Junior SQA Automation Engineer in Automation Solutionz, a Canada based software firm who provides testing solutions for large scale software and mobile applications. Mr. Towshik Anam Taj is currently serving as an adjunct lecturer in the same department and has experience on teaching a broader range of courses starting from Introduction to Computer Science, Digital Logic Design to teaching advanced level courses such as Artificial Intelligence, Computer Networks etc. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Cyber Security, Cryptography and other subdomains of the mentioned research areas. He has published research articles in the area of Artificial Intelligence and Cryptography under prestigious conferences and journals and he is currently working on numerous research projects which are exploring various research domains such as Computer Vision, Machine Learning associated Cyber Security etc.

name: Umme Jannat Taposhi
bio: Umme Jannat Taposhi is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh. She earned both her Bachelor’s degree (Fall 2023) and Master’s degree (Summer 2025) in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, graduating with the Highest Distinction in both. Her research interests lie at the intersection of: Taposhi’s work focuses on understanding how people interact with intelligent technologies across social, educational, and healthcare contexts. She is particularly interested in designing data-driven yet human-centered systems that improve usability, inclusivity, and trust. Through her teaching and research, she aims to bridge the gap between human behavior and computational intelligence to create more inclusive and impactful digital experiences.

name: Yasmin Nadia
bio: Yasmin Nadia graduated from Brac University in 2023 with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering. She began her professional journey as a Software Engineer Trainee at BJIT Ltd., where she worked with the MERN stack from August 2023 to November 2023. From October 2023, she served as an Adjunct Lecturer, teaching Data Communication, Discrete Mathematics, Computer Networks Theories, Object-Oriented Programming, and Microprocessor Labs. In October 2025, she joined as a Lecturer, continuing to teach the same subjects. Her research interests include deep learning, computer vision, multimodal learning, and conditional content generation, with applications in neurological disorder detection and healthcare AI.
